steps.title: Use the negative solution
steps.content: The negative solution is $-\sqrt{c^{2}+39^{2}}$. Values $x=\pm c$ would make the denominator zero, so they are not valid.

choiceElimination:
• Choice A — $-c$: Substituting $x=-c$ gives $\sqrt{(-c)^{2}-c^{2}}=\sqrt{0}=0$ in the denominator, making the equation undefined. ✗
  • Choice B — $-{c}^{2}-{39}^{2}$: This is a large negative number. Squaring it gives $(c^{2}+39^{2})^{2}$, not $c^{2}+39^{2}$, so it does not satisfy $x^{2}=c^{2}+39^{2}$. ✗
  • Choice C — $-\sqrt{{39}^{2}-{c}^{2}}$: This would require $x^{2}=39^{2}-c^{2}$, but we derived $x^{2}=c^{2}+39^{2}$. These differ unless $c=0$, contradicting c being a positive constant. ✗
  • Choice D — $-\sqrt{{c}^{2}+{39}^{2}}$: Gives $x^{2}=c^{2}+39^{2}$, matching the derived solution exactly. ✓
That matches Choice D — $-\sqrt{{c}^{2}+{39}^{2}}$.
